meaning: n.抽筋,腹部绞痛,(pl.)月经痛adj.狭窄的,难解的vt.使抽筋,以铁箍扣紧
examples: Hillsden was complaining of a cramp in his calf muscles.
Like more and more women, she believes wedlock would cramp her style.
It is all an attitude, and one day the attitude will become a weird cramp, a pain, and then it will collapse.
And while windsurfing, Alfredo developed a cramp and was unable to swim back to the boat.
Signing up to such an offer must have caused painful writer's cramp in some Arab capitals.
